equivalence_note: f3 section 2.2: a -w file watch desugars to a -F path=... -F perm=... exit-list syscall rule. The two rules below express the SAME audit intent and the cost model MUST assign them the SAME tier, direction, byte shape (900 B file-watch event), and rate band. The TRAP is treating the watch form and the syscall form as different cost.

rules.note: Single-file write+attr watch. -p wa = AUDIT_PERM_WRITE (26 syscalls) + AUDIT_PERM_ATTR (23 syscalls) on this path (permtab.h, f3 section 2.4). 900 B/event (file-watch, no EXECVE).
EQUIVALENT desugared form of the watch above (f3 section 2.2). Identical underlying perm-group coverage and identical 900 B/event file-watch shape. MUST be priced equal to the -w form.

rate_band_basis: Single-file write/attr access on /etc/passwd; modeled like the #11 passwd_write band (low100/typ2000/high20000). The two forms share the band by construction.
equivalence_assertion: rate_band[watch] == rate_band[syscall]; bytesPerEvent[watch] == bytesPerEvent[syscall] == 900; tier and direction equal. A cost impl that prices them differently is WRONG.
